seoTitle: How to Format Text & Change Font Size in Squarespace
metaDescription: Learn how to format text and adjust font size in Squarespace for a cleaner, more readable site that keeps your branding consistent across every page.
intro: Text formatting is one of the fastest ways to make a Squarespace site look more polished and easier to read. This walkthrough covers how to adjust font styles, line height, and size so your content matches your brand and stays consistent from page to page. Whether you're building a blog or a portfolio, getting the basics right here pays off across the whole site.

steps.heading: Open the text settings
steps.body: Head into the block or page where you want to make changes and access the text formatting options in the Squarespace editor.

steps.heading: Adjust font size
steps.body: Use the sizing controls to scale headings and body copy so they feel proportionate and easy to scan on both desktop and mobile.

steps.heading: Set line height and style
steps.body: Fine-tune line height and font style to improve readability, especially for longer blocks of text like blog posts or product descriptions.

steps.heading: Apply formatting consistently
steps.body: Carry the same font choices across different text elements on your site so your typography reinforces your brand identity.

steps.heading: Preview your changes
steps.body: Check how the updated formatting looks in preview mode before publishing, comparing desktop and mobile views.

faq.question: Will changing font size affect my whole site or just one page?
faq.answer: It depends on where you make the change. Editing an individual text block only affects that block, while site-wide style settings apply everywhere that font is used.

faq.question: Can I use custom fonts in Squarespace?
faq.answer: Squarespace offers a range of built-in font pairings, and formatting settings let you adjust size and style within those options to match your brand.

faq.question: Does font formatting affect page load speed?
faq.answer: Standard text formatting changes like size and style have minimal impact on load speed since they're handled through Squarespace's native styling tools.

faq.question: How do I keep formatting consistent across my whole site?
faq.answer: Apply the same font choices to each text element type, like headings and body text, so the styling stays uniform as you build out new pages.
